content:
🌟 smart-money-concepts | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide traders with Python-based tools to implement and backtest algorithmic trading strategies using Smart Money Concepts (SMC) and ICT methodologies.
✨ Key Features: • Fair Value Gap (FVG) indicator • Swing Highs and Lows detection • Break of Structure (BOS) and Change of Character (CHoCH) indicators • Order Blocks (OB) detection
📖 Summary: This Python package implements Inner Circle Trader (ICT) smart money concepts for algorithmic trading. It provides a suite of indicators like Order Blocks, Liquidity, Fair Value Gaps, and market structure breaks to help traders analyze market sentiment and identify potential trading opportunities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 pytermgui | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Developing complex, visually appealing, and interactive terminal user interfaces with ease.
✨ Key Features: • Modular widget system • Mouse support • Customizable and rapid terminal markup language (TIM) • YAML or Python-based styling engines • Desktop-inspired window manager system
📖 Summary: PyTermGUI is a Python framework for building rich and interactive terminal user interfaces (TUIs). It offers a modular widget system, mouse support, and a customizable markup language for styling text, enabling the creation of desktop-like applications within the terminal.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 PyPDFForm | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating the process of filling and managing data within PDF forms using Python.
✨ Key Features: • Inspect PDF form fields • Fill PDF forms using Python dictionaries • Create new form fields on PDFs • Extract pages from PDFs • Merge multiple PDF documents
📖 Summary: PyPDFForm is a Python library designed for automating PDF form interactions. It allows users to programmatically inspect, fill, and create form fields within PDF documents, simplifying form processing tasks.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 cursor-clip | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a visually intuitive and dynamically positioned clipboard history manager on Wayland compositors, enhancing productivity and clipboard management.
✨ Key Features: • Windows 11-style clipboard history • Dynamic positioning at cursor location • Native GNOME/Libadwaita design • Advanced Wayland integration (Layer Shell Protocol, Cursor Tracking) • Multi-output and Multi-input support
📖 Summary: Cursor Clip is a modern Wayland clipboard manager built with Rust and GTK4, offering a Windows 11-style clipboard history that dynamically positions itself at the mouse cursor. It integrates seamlessly with the GNOME design language and Wayland protocols for reliable clipboard handling.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
